body: This PR introduces CATKE for RK3. Substepping is not supported at the moment. I am trying to reproduce figures 15 and 16 of [Wagner et al](https://agupubs.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/epdf/10.1029/2024MS004522), I think the LES data I have might be wrong, so disregard the LES, but just looking at the time-step dependency, it looks like RK3 does help a bit with decreasing the dependency with respect to vanilla AB2. @glwagner, what do you think? Maybe if it is quick, we can try doing a recalibration with RK3? The solutions seem to be slightly different in the case of AB2 vs RK3 (probably very slightly) ## Vanilla AB2 (`tke_time_step = nothing`) [ab2_step.pdf](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21685527/ab2_step.pdf) ## Substepped AB2 (`tke_time_step = 2minutes`) [ab2_step_2min.pdf](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21685526/ab2_step_2min.pdf) ## Split RKE (`tke_time_step` does not matter) [rk3_step.pdf](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21733565/rk3_step.pdf) Another option is to remove the `time_step_tke!` equation in case of an RK3 and add a term in the tendency computation like I am doing in this branch: https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/blob/ss/omip-branch/src/TurbulenceClosures/turbulence_closure_implementations/TKEBasedVerticalDiffusivities/time_step_catke_equation.jl

labels.description: So things don't blow up and boil the lobsters alive
labels.name: numerics 🧮

title: RK3 CATKE Implementation
